rationale: Y engages more directly with the ground truth's two most-repeated concerns (the platform-wide scope of the default-priority change, and the requested P1+P2-vs-main perf comparison), even citing perf numbers (-26.9%, -14.1%) that match the actual PR thread. But Y's cited code locations for the RMSNorm swap sites (qwen_image_transformer.py:640-643, :1140) are demonstrably wrong against the diff's own hunk headers (actual lines are ~557-558, 595-596, 1009), which undercuts trust in its other unverifiable file/line claims. X's findings are fewer and cover less of the ground-truth thread, but every cited line number matches the diff exactly, and its two flagged issues (misleading test name covering a non-inductor case, dropped docstring rationale) are valid, precise, and directly actionable.
